title: Sarepta CEO Signals Intent to Expand ELEVIDYS to Younger Duchenne Patients at JPM
text:
At the JPMorgan Healthcare Conference in January 2024, Sarepta Therapeutics CEO Doug Ingram signaled the company's strategic intent to expand ELEVIDYS to younger patient populations beyond the initial 4-5 year old indication. This expansion would represent a significant market opportunity and validation of the gene therapy platform.

Initial Label: ELEVIDYS had received accelerated approval for treatment of ambulatory pediatric patients aged 4 through 5 years with Duchenne muscular dystrophy following its June 2023 approval. This initial population represented only a portion of the total addressable market.

Expansion Strategy: Sarepta was pursuing label expansion to include older ambulatory patients, with the goal of making the therapy available to the vast majority of Duchenne patients in the United States. The June 2024 label expansion achieved this goal, making ELEVIDYS available to over 80% of Duchenne patients.

Clinical Evidence: The company was building evidence of ELEVIDYS efficacy in broader patient populations. Three-year EMBARK trial follow-up data demonstrated sustained functional improvements across multiple measures. Secondary endpoints were particularly strong despite primary endpoint miss.

Commercial Timing: The label expansion approval in June 2024 came at an optimal time, allowing the company to capitalize on strong ELEVIDYS momentum. Q2 earnings showed over 1,000 antibody screening kit requests within weeks of approval.

Peak Market Opportunity: Expanding ELEVIDYS to 80%+ of DMD patients positioned the company for peak annual sales estimates of $2.7 billion to over $5 billion, depending on adoption rates and pricing.
